audit_status.source_base: Greek Acts text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Iconium signs/opposition, Lystra healing, Zeus/Hermes misunderstanding, living Divine speech, stoning Paul, strengthening disciples, appointing elders.

verses.restored: It happened in Iconium that they entered together into the synagogue of the Judeans and spoke in such a way that a great multitude of both Judeans and Greeks trusted.
But the Judeans who were disobedient stirred up and embittered the souls/lives of the nations against the siblings.
Therefore they remained a considerable time, speaking boldly in the Lord, who was bearing witness to the word of his grace by giving signs and wonders to happen through their hands.
The multitude of the city was divided: some were with the Judeans, and some with the apostles.
When an impulse arose from both the nations and Judeans, with their rulers, to mistreat and stone them,
becoming aware, they fled to the cities of Lycaonia, Lystra and Derbe, and the surrounding region,
and there they were announcing the good news.
In Lystra a certain man was sitting, powerless in the feet, lame from his mother's womb, who had never walked.
This one heard Paul speaking. Paul, looking intently at him and seeing that he had trust to be saved/healed,
said with a great voice, Stand upright on your feet. And he leaped up and began walking.
The crowds, seeing what Paul did, lifted their voice in Lycaonian, saying, The gods, becoming like humans, have come down to us.
They were calling Barnabas Zeus, and Paul Hermes, because he was the leading speaker.
The priest of Zeus, whose temple was before the city, brought bulls and garlands to the gates, wanting to sacrifice with the crowds.
But the apostles Barnabas and Paul, hearing this, tore their garments and rushed out into the crowd, crying out

and saying, Men, why do you do these things? We also are humans of like suffering with you, announcing good news to you to turn from these empty things to the living Divine, who made heaven and earth and sea and all things in them.
In past generations he allowed all the nations to walk in their ways,
yet he did not leave himself without witness, doing good, giving you rains from heaven and fruitful seasons, filling your hearts with food and gladness.
Saying these things, with difficulty they restrained the crowds from sacrificing to them.
But Judeans came from Antioch and Iconium, and persuading the crowds, they stoned Paul and dragged him outside the city, supposing him to have died.
But when the disciples surrounded him, rising, he entered the city. The next day he went out with Barnabas to Derbe.
Having announced the good news to that city and made many disciples, they returned to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch,
strengthening the souls/lives of the disciples, encouraging them to remain in the trust, and saying that through many pressures it is necessary for us to enter the reign of the Divine.
Appointing elders for them in every assembly, with prayer and fasting, they entrusted them to the Lord in whom they had trusted.
Passing through Pisidia, they came into Pamphylia.
Speaking the word in Perga, they went down to Attalia.
From there they sailed away to Antioch, from where they had been handed over to the grace of the Divine for the work they had fulfilled.
Arriving and gathering the assembly, they reported all that the Divine had done with them, and that he had opened a door of trust to the nations.
They remained no little time with the disciples.
